behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between diff extraction and review-input construction modules. A unit test of one helper can miss wiring mistakes (e.g., passing full file blobs instead of hunks). Real network/OS is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixture PR diff where unchanged lines contain obvious lint/bug bait text. Run the real diff-fetch-parser + prompt/input-builder pipeline in-process, then assert the produced review payload includes only changed hunks (and excludes unchanged sections).

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correct diff-position anchoring depends on GitHub PR review API semantics. In-process fakes often accept invalid/shifted positions, hiding real placement failures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real test PR, run comment-posting against GitHub, then fetch posted review comments and verify each anchors to expected file + diff line in the PR Files Changed view.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment (defect detection quality), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by pure wiring tests.
behaviors.test_description: Use a seeded PR with a known bug. Run the real model-based reviewer and assert at least one finding maps to the known defect class/location; evaluate pass-rate over repeated runs (e.g., >=X/10) to account for stochasticity.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module consistency invariant between inline-comment results and summary assembly. It is primarily orchestration/data-contract logic, not inherently dependent on real OS/network/model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline with mocked transport boundaries but real orchestration: collect attempted inline posts and generated summary, then assert one-to-one correspondence (stable IDs/file-line keys) and no summary-only items.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end agent pipeline behavior across real PR I/O plus model-driven review and summarization steps. Multiple agentic steps in sequence are the explicit failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, execute the full automation unattended: fetch diff, generate findings, post inline comments, post summary. Assert successful completion, expected artifact creation/order, and no human retry/manual patching.
